bookings.additionalNotes: Kim is a rescue spaniel and gets stressed in unfamiliar situations. Last year all 3 shared together but his constant pacing and circling in a kennel environment stressed out Millie and she was very intolerant of him towards the end of their stay. This was their first time all together as we had only had him for 8 months - Milo and Millie are use to sharing together which is why I have put them together and Kim on his own. Milo and Millie are not big fans of other dogs - Millie especially with bigger dogs. They are trained on a whistle and Millie is Grade 2 Gundog. Milo is obsessed with a ball - will do anything for one, but reluctant to give it up. Kim is a food stealer, Millie is fussy with food.

bookings.feedingInstructions: Core Kibbles Twice daily (400grams daily). Harry gets a little extra to keep weight maintained.
bookings.medicalInstructions: No medical problems with any of the dogs.
bookings.additionalNotes:
Harry the black lab is very energetic, he will leap any wall or child gate. very excitable - has the waggiest tail and bum (hence why his weight is much less than his brothers). We tend to give him a bit extra food to keep his weight up, also give him potatoes if going spare to help maintain weight. He is the softest and most loveable of the boys. 
Clive the fox red, is fairly calm. He acts shy and reserved till he gets a fuss then he is anyone’s. Clive is better in the company of his brothers. Gets a little over excited at times and likes to try and dominate Harry. He is a chewer and has been destructive with his beds if he doesn’t get enough exercise.
Woody, blonde lab - is a cheeky chappie, that back answers you when you pretend to chase him. He loves to carry sticks (hence his name) the bigger the branch the better and also likes to chew. He is a barker which sets Clive off. Woody does not like his paws touched but loves a tickle everywhere else. 
All the boys tend to cosy up together, but Woody can sometimes be grumpy towards Harry and Clive if they disturb his naps.

bookings.feedingInstructions: Food will be provided, he gets fed at supper time
bookings.medicalInstructions:
He usually gets 1 x synoquin, 3 x pardale v, every morning, wrapped in ham
Then the same again at night, but with 1 x prednicare on top
We use protexin twice daily if his stools are loose and usually clean his anal area with pre-made solution and dog wipes and apply savlon gel every evening as he has an anal anemola that we are maintaining to prevent/minimise infection. Is this something that you can also do
bookings.additionalNotes: Caesar is 12 and will be 13 on 23rd May, he’s started to struggle with his back legs, more so with stairs/steps/sofa etc, his stomach is more delicate and is on a chicken and rice diet with additional vitamins to try and ensure his stool remains firmer to reduce previous issues with anal glands, he’s also quite fussy at times but we don’t have an issue with him feeding on this. Some butchers dry food and treats will also be provided. Caesar will most like be upset and mop as we are leaving him but our son is getting married and all the family will be at the wedding, so no one to look after him in his own home, especially with the additional needs in regards to medication and cleaning.

bookings.feedingInstructions: One bowl of food in the morning and usually left for him to eat as and when it suits him.
We will provide Emiko's food for her she would get a bag in the morning and the other at supper time. We also give her 1/2 carrot after walks and will provide these too.
bookings.additionalNotes:
Kouki is still a bit hyper and wants to be friends with everyone and wants to go walks  a lot- he has lots of energy and can become over excited. Kouki loves to play fetch and will give you his ball.  Kouki has selective hearing when it comes to recall. Kouki has quite a high prey drive and will chase birds but not cats (we have 3 who gets on with well with). Kouki is an intact male.
Emiko was attacked by another dog whilst on a walk so she tends to be more reserved- she is a lazy lump but sometimes needs a bit of encouragement to go for walks  but she enjoys going once she has her harness on. Emiko will bark until she is happy to see you - very food orientated. Emiko will bark to defend her food from others. She will hopefully be spayed later this year.
They are both very cuddly and love bosies. They cast a LOT.
They both cuddle up well together and usually settle down after a walk and will generally  sleep  for a good while afterwards.
Once one barks the other follows- and Kouki tends to bark at his own shadow at times.
Any problems please phone.

bookings.additionalNotes:
Does try to extend boundaries but accepts secured boundaries. Eg. My daughters garden has hedging, so Ruby will squeeze through to next door, smell of their dog to enticing, maybe. In friends gardens, walled fenced,gated, does accept but has me in sight. Don't know reaction if she is unable to see me.  Does respond to whistle and recall quite good.
Words to know : down ( meaning off) , sit, come ( arm out point to ankle) . 'This way,' to get her to follow you.

bookings.additionalNotes: moving to lapdog and no 14

Note: Zelda can be sometimes be aggressive towards thor if he steals a toy or if he is getting more attention from us she can sometimes snap at him, however they get on fine 99% of the time, she can just be a bit jealous at times. Thor tends to run away and cry if she is being a grump towards him! Regardless Zelda rarely leaves his side and when in out and about or in new places she is shy and quiet and looks for him. Zelda does not like being with other dogs she tends to hide behind us on walks if she sees another dog coming however if she is out walking with thor she will bark at them. Brother and Sister Thor and Zelda are both loving, affectionate dogs who loves their home comforts! They are used to sleeping on our bed at home and love nothing more than a cuddle and some company. Thor loves playing with other dogs, Zelda does not and will hide and stay by me when thor is playing with other dogs. When we are not home, Zelda is crate trained and stays in her crate and thor sleeps in the kitchen alongside her but not in the crate with her. Recall and being off lead is not their strong points if they see something they want to chase after - be it a rabbit or a bird, they will most likely be off - thor loves a chase! They have been to your kennels many times before just adding this info incase.
